content_markdown: Which of the following combinations of changes will produce the greatest increase in the theoretical maximum efficiency of a heat engine?

diagram_metadata.description: A table showing four combinations of changes to the temperature of the cold reservoir and the temperature of the hot reservoir.

options.label: A
options.text: Temperature of cold reservoir: Increase; Temperature of hot reservoir: Decrease

options.label: B
options.text: Temperature of cold reservoir: Increase; Temperature of hot reservoir: Increase

options.label: C
options.text: Temperature of cold reservoir: Decrease; Temperature of hot reservoir: Decrease

options.label: D
options.text: Temperature of cold reservoir: Decrease; Temperature of hot reservoir: Increase

vibe_explanation: The theoretical maximum efficiency of a heat engine is given by the Carnot efficiency formula: $\eta = 1 - \frac{T_{cold}}{T_{hot}}$. To maximize this value, we need to make the fraction $\frac{T_{cold}}{T_{hot}}$ as small as possible. This is achieved by decreasing the numerator ($T_{cold}$) and increasing the denominator ($T_{hot}$).
